title: senna stipulacea var. anglorum
intro: Senna stipulacea var. anglorum, also known as English senna or Stipulacea senna, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family, commonly used for its medicinal and laxative properties. The plant is rich in anthraquinone glycosides, which are responsible for its purgative effects, making it a popular remedy for constipation and other digestive issues. The medicinal properties of Senna stipulacea var. anglorum include its ability to stimulate bowel movements, relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tea or capsules, can be made by drying and processing the leaves and pods, which can be used to support digestive health and promote regular bowel movements.
